title: NOC 82031 – Contractors and supervisors, landscaping, grounds maintenance and horticulture services | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors and contractors under NOC 82031 are the technical leaders of Canada’s urban and residential green spaces. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals coordinate high-stakes projects ranging from complex landscape construction (hardscaping) to specialized horticulture and large-scale grounds maintenance. In 2026, as Canadian cities prioritize "Green Infrastructure" and climate-resilient urban planning, these supervisors are in high demand to lead crews in sustainable planting and advanced irrigation techniques. For immigration, this role is a premier candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and the BC PNP Skills Immigration stream.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level2Major Group82 – Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related productionSub-Major Group820 – Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related productionMinor Group8203 – Contractors and supervisors, agriculture, horticulture and aquacultureNOC Code82031

duties: Supervise, coordinate, and schedule the activities of workers who maintain lawns, gardens, athletic fields, golf courses, and cemeteries.
Manage the construction of landscape features such as walkways, retaining walls, decks, and irrigation systems.
Establish work schedules and procedures and coordinate activities with other sub-contractors or departments.
Requisition materials and supplies and manage the maintenance of specialized equipment like excavators, skid steers, and hydroseeders.
Resolve work-related problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and landscape quality.
Train workers in job duties, advanced safety procedures (e.g., Working at Heights), and the use of new horticulture technology.
Maintain production and quality control records and prepare cost estimates for landscaping contracts.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Work Experience: Several years of experience as a landscape or grounds maintenance laborer are mandatory.
Education: A college diploma or university degree in horticulture, landscape architecture, or a related field is often required for large-scale commercial contractors.
Certifications: Provincial certification as a Landscape Horticulturist (Red Seal) is available and highly recommended for career progression.
Licenses: A pesticide application license and a valid driver's license (often Class 3 or 5) are typically required.
salary_prospects: Note: Contractors who own their own firms have significantly higher earning potential through commercial and municipal contracts.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector is experiencing a "Professionalization" phase. As municipal environmental regulations tighten, the need for supervisors who understand carbon-sequestering plants and low-impact development (LID) is growing. Retirements in the public sector (Parks and Recreation) are also creating high-stability openings.
labour_market_demand: The demand for landscaping and horticulture supervisors is Very Good, particularly in provinces with high residential growth and tourism infrastructure. In 2026, Ontario (GTA), British Columbia (Lower Mainland), and Quebec are facing significant shortages of skilled supervisors to manage seasonal and year-round maintenance contracts. With the rise of "Smart Landscaping"—which integrates IoT-controlled lighting and moisture sensors—Canadian firms are actively seeking international talent with modern technical backgrounds.
Green Economy Leadership: Lead projects in a country that values sustainable urban forestry and high-end residential landscaping.
Entrepreneurial Opportunities: This NOC has a high rate of self-employment, making it ideal for those looking to eventually start their own landscaping or snow-clearing business.
Year-Round Operations: Transition from summer landscaping to winter snow and ice management, providing stable year-round income in most provinces.
PR Strategic Pathway: A high-priority "Trade" occupation that frequently receives targeted invitations in provincial draws.
pathways: Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A reliable route for supervisors with the required work experience and a job offer.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for supervisors supporting urban development and regional tourism (golf courses/resorts).
Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): Targeted via the Foreign Worker stream for skilled trades.
Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted for candidates with experience in "Trade" occupations.
